description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:18-buster Docker image with three stages: build, package, and notify, and a before_script that exports PREVIOUS_TAGS, LAST_TAG, VERSION (default 2.0.0), PATCH, LAST_COMMIT_SUBJECT, LAST_COMMIT_BODY and runs yarn to install dependencies. In the build stage add a build job that runs yarn run build and stores the dist/ directory as a one‑day artifact. In the package stage add crx, crx-alpha, crx-beta, xpi-beta, and xpi-unsigned jobs, each depending on the build job, executing yarn run manifest:specify and yarn run build-zip (or npx web-ext sign for xpi-beta), producing zip or xpi files under dist-zip/ that expire after one week, with crx limited to tags, crx-alpha limited to the feature/chrome-manifest-v3 branch and excluded when the commit message matches /NO-CI/, crx-beta limited to develop and also excluded on /NO-CI/, and xpi-beta limited to develop, requiring SIGN_FF_API_KEY and excluded on /NO-CI/. The xpi-beta job runs npx web-ext sign with the provided API key and secret. Finally, in the notify stage add alpha_notify and beta_notify jobs that depend on crx-alpha and xpi-beta respectively, run yarn run discord:alpha or discord:beta with the version, patch, Discord ID, token, commit subject and body, and are executed only on their respective branches unless the corresponding Discord ID variable is missing or the commit message contains NO-CI.
